rationale: In the citing paper, Shu and Gneezy (2010) is not only listed in background; it is invoked in the episode-level findings discussion to explain why WFF effects are stronger near the end of a series. The citation supplies a specific behavioral interpretation tied to procrastination/completion under temporal structure, which the authors use to make sense of their observed pattern. Although the use is brief and not methodological, it meaningfully informs interpretation rather than serving as a generic mention.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Shu and Gneezy's insight that temporal structuring can reduce procrastination of enjoyable activities to interpret why wait-based promotion may help users continue through serialized content, including later episodes.
